subjects.name: Mathematics
subjects.notes: Ceiling performance. Maintenance only. Method (YouTube → worked examples → problems) is transferable to other subjects.

subjects.name: History
subjects.notes: Confidence well-calibrated. Protect, do not neglect.

subjects.name: English Home Language
subjects.notes: Confidence (5-6) is below mark (76). He underestimates his ability here. Easy lift via past papers and reassurance — no rebuild needed, just evidence.

subjects.name: Afrikaans First Additional Language
subjects.notes: Confidence matches mark — not a blind spot. Real gap requiring active work. Lower APS leverage than LO admin completion despite feeling like the priority.

subjects.name: Physical Sciences
subjects.notes: Largest content gap. Confidence tracks mark — not a blind spot but a genuine rebuild. Solo studying does not work here. Peer session required. Target 55-60% by end of year.

subjects.name: Life Orientation
subjects.notes: Exam component is fine. Community Service admin/logging is completely uncompleted. Estimated 15-20 APS-relevant marks unclaimed. Highest leverage APS play on the entire profile — requires no learning, only task completion. This is a Saturday fix, not a semester rebuild.

aps.computation_note: Based on IEB APS scale: 90-100=7, 80-89=6, 70-79=5, 60-69=4, 50-59=3, 40-49=2, 30-39=1. Maths(7) + CAT(7) + History(6) + English(6) + Afrikaans(3) + Sciences(2) = 31. LO excluded pending admin completion. LO completion expected to add material points.
aps.aps_at_full_lo: TBC — depends on final LO admin completion
aps.highest_leverage_action: LO community service admin completion (no learning curve)

d1_learning_modality.description: Learns by watching worked examples first, then attempting problems. Not a reader-first learner. Textbook-led studying without video scaffold is low yield for him.
d1_learning_modality.action: Every new topic: find a YouTube explainer first. Attempt problems only after watching. Do not start with the textbook.

d2_focus_energy.crash_pattern: focus collapses at point of not-knowing, not at time limit
d2_focus_energy.recovery: short break — extending session does not work
d2_focus_energy.action: Phone in another room for the 9pm block. Pre-decide response to wall-hitting: flag it, skip it, photo to a mate. Session-killers don't kill sessions when the exit is pre-built.

d3_procrastination.onset: starts but loses momentum within 20 minutes on heavy subjects
d3_procrastination.avoidance_detail: Not boredom, not pressure — specifically the 'I don't know what to do next' feeling triggers avoidance
d3_procrastination.plan_implication: Plans built on 'study for X hours' will fail. Plans built on appointments, external deadlines, and co-study slots will work.
d3_procrastination.action: Replace time-based goals with event-based commitments. 'Tuesdays 8pm I study Sciences with [name]' outperforms 'I'll study Sciences 3x a week'.

d4_confidence_map.calibration_note: Rav's confidence ratings track his marks closely across almost every subject. Unusual self-awareness — not a blind-spot profile.
d4_confidence_map.leading_indicator_target: Physical Sciences confidence moves from 3-4 to 5-6 by remeasure date

d5_defence_mechanism.description: When hitting an unknown question, first move is to ask a mate who gets it
d5_defence_mechanism.peer_network_note: Has a genuine go-to mate in every main subject including Sciences — unusually well-networked
d5_defence_mechanism.weakness: Mates unavailable at 11pm when the wall hits. On-demand help fails at peak study time.
d5_defence_mechanism.intervention_design: Scheduled peer sessions, not on-demand requests
d5_defence_mechanism.action: Book a standing Sciences session with a named mate. Same time every week. Not 'let me know if you need help' — a calendar appointment.

stakes_awareness.reframe_note: LO community service completion outperforms Afrikaans in APS-per-hour — zero learning curve, pure task completion

accountability_preference.what_works_detail: Co-study, in-person or FaceTime — another human present or on-call
accountability_preference.action: Two fixed co-study slots per week: Sciences + one other weak subject. Same time, same place or call. Binary: happened or it didn't.

d7_honesty_flag.response: yes — there is something about matric not shared with parents or teachers
d7_honesty_flag.follow_up: also reported: does not tell anyone when stressed; considers this acceptable
d7_honesty_flag.escalation: not_required — surfaced and acknowledged; not scored; Desi's territory
d7_honesty_flag.note_for_codebase: Do not display this dimension publicly. Include only in parent/guardian view if that tier is built.

archetype.name: The Calibrated Outperformer
archetype.tagline: You know exactly where you stand. The problem is you keep trying to fix it alone — and alone is the one mode you told us doesn't work.
archetype.insight: You're not avoiding Sciences because you're lazy. You're avoiding it because hitting a wall alone at 11pm with no clear next move is a genuinely bad feeling, and your brain has learned to route around it. The fix isn't willpower. It's removing the solo.
archetype.strength: Self-honesty. Rav's confidence ratings match his marks almost exactly — he's not delusional about his position, which makes intervention design far easier.
archetype.risk: Solo-running a problem that requires co-presence. High self-awareness without structural support becomes 'I know what's wrong' paralysis.
archetype.highest_leverage_action: LO admin completion (Saturday fix, no learning curve, material APS impact)
archetype.second_highest_leverage_action: Standing peer session for Physical Sciences (structural, not motivational fix)

this_week.action: Complete LO community service logging and admin
this_week.rationale: Highest APS return per hour on the entire profile. Pure task completion — no learning required.

this_week.action: Book a named, standing Sciences co-study session with a specific mate
this_week.rationale: Peer-learner defence mechanism requires structural availability, not on-demand requests
this_week.timing: This week — calendar slot with notification

this_week.action: Apply the Maths method to one Physics or Chemistry topic
this_week.rationale: YouTube explainer → 5 problems → repeat. He already owns this method. Redirect it.

this_week.action: Phone in another room for the 9pm study block
this_week.rationale: Phone proximity is a named session-killer. Removal, not DND.

four_week_milestone.target: Physical Sciences confidence moves from 3-4 to 5-6 on the same 1-10 scale
four_week_milestone.measure: Re-run Dimension 4 confidence map on all 7 subjects
four_week_milestone.leading_indicator_note: Confidence shift precedes mark shift. If confidence moves, the intervention is working before results confirm it.
